verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED_BY: {source}is permitted only after the role reviews the work plan, confirms ground conditions, and grants clearance for {target} to proceed in the designated work zone.
verbGlossary.MOBILIZED_BY: {source}is transported to the jobsite and positioned for use under the direction of the specified {target}, who authorizes the crane plan and lift schedule.
verbGlossary.RIGGED_BY: {source} is connected to loads via slings, shackles, or spreader bars by {target}, who directs each pick according to the lift plan.
verbGlossary.INSPECTED_BY: {source} undergoes a formal examination by {target} to verify compliance with safety regulations, project specifications, or quality standards before use on site.
verbGlossary.OPERATED_BY: {source} or instrument is physically controlled and run by the specified trade or {target} during active construction operations.
verbGlossary.GRADED_TO_SPEC_BY: {source}is verified against design elevations and tolerances by {target}, who checks cut/fill grades with survey data and approves the subgrade.
verbGlossary.STAGED_BY: {source} or materials are pre-positioned at the point of installation by {target} to maintain workflow and minimize double-handling on the jobsite.
verbGlossary.CALIBRATED_BY: {source} is tested and adjusted to ensure measurement accuracy by {target}, typically before concrete pours, welding operations, or testing procedures.
verbGlossary.LOAD_TESTED_BY: {source} or installed element undergoes a proof-load or capacity test directed by {target} to confirm it meets the design load requirements.
verbGlossary.TAGGED_BY: {source} receives a physical inspection tag (green/yellow/red) from {target} indicating its operational status and next-inspection date per site safety protocol.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED_BY: {source} or welding procedure is formally qualified by {target} through testing and documentation, producing a certificate required before production work begins.
verbGlossary.ROUTED_BY: {source} are laid out and connected along a path determined by {target}, ensuring code-compliant distribution of power, water, or other services across the site.
verbGlossary.DISPATCHED_BY: {source} is assigned to a specific work area or task by {target}, who allocates equipment or labor based on the day's production schedule.
verbGlossary.BATCH_TESTED_BY: {source} samples from a delivered batch are tested by {target} using break tests, slump tests, or other QC methods to verify mix-design compliance.
verbGlossary.PLACED_BY: {source} is physically installed or deposited in its final location on the project by the specified trade or {target}, following approved placement procedures.
verbGlossary.DETAILED_BY: {source} quantities, bends, and lap lengths are calculated and drawn by {target} to produce shop drawings or installation details from the design documents.
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED_BY: {source} properties, grades, and performance criteria are defined by {target} in project specifications or engineering calculations before procurement.
verbGlossary.PROCURED_BY: {source} is sourced, bid, purchased, and delivery-scheduled by {target} through the project's procurement workflow, including vendor qualification and lead-time tracking.
verbGlossary.TAKEOFF_BY: {source} quantities are measured and counted from drawings by {target} to produce an accurate bill of materials for estimating and procurement.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED_BY: {source} data sheets, samples, or shop drawings are formally transmitted to the design team for approval by {target} through the submittal process.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED_BY: {source}is functionally tested, performance-verified, and formally accepted by {target} through a structured commissioning protocol.
verbGlossary.CLASH_CHECKED_BY: {source} routing or placement is verified against the coordinated BIM model by {target} to identify and resolve spatial conflicts before installation.
verbGlossary.CATALOGED_BY: {source} submittals, spec sheets, and delivery records are logged and filed by {target} into the project document management system for retrieval and audit.
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED_BY: {source} deliveries and installations are ordered in the project schedule by {target} to align with construction phasing and avoid trade stacking.
verbGlossary.APPROVED_BY: {source} selection, finish, or mockup is formally accepted by {target}, authorizing the contractor to proceed with fabrication or bulk procurement.
verbGlossary.CODE_REVIEWED_BY: {source} or assembly is evaluated against applicable building codes and fire-rating requirements by {target} to confirm regulatory compliance before installation.
verbGlossary.BALANCED_BY: {source}is tested and adjusted by {target} to achieve the specified airflow, water flow, or temperature distribution across all zones.
verbGlossary.STARTUP_TESTED_BY: Installed equipment undergoes initial energization and functional testing by {target} to verify safe operation and readiness for commissioning.
verbGlossary.REPORTS_TO: {source} professional provides daily activity reports, safety observations, or inspection results to the supervising {target} as part of the project chain of command.
verbGlossary.SUPERVISED_BY: {source} receives direct task assignments, work-method instructions, and quality oversight from the specified {target} on the jobsite.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED_BY: {source} work is scheduled and sequenced alongside adjacent trades by {target} to prevent conflicts and maintain production flow in shared work areas.
verbGlossary.DIRECTED_BY: {source} receives specific work assignments and installation instructions from {target}, who holds authority over the crew's daily production.
verbGlossary.MONITORED_BY: {source}'s activities are observed for safety compliance by {target}, who can issue stop-work orders for unsafe conditions.
verbGlossary.PROOF_ROLLED_BY: {source}is tested by driving loaded equipment across it under the observation of {target}, who checks for deflection indicating inadequate compaction.
verbGlossary.FIELD_VERIFIED_BY: {source}is physically checked in the field by {target} against approved drawings to confirm correct placement, thickness, or coverage.
verbGlossary.UNIT_PRICED_BY: {source} cost is broken down into per-unit rates by {target} for cost tracking, progress billing, and earned-value analysis against the budget.
